| MCP:8Un92O | # SpringBoot Java Template | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | [![Twitter](https://img.shields.io/twitter/url?style=social&url=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Fgeminikims)](https://twitter.com/geminikims) | MCP:8Un92O | [![Youtube](https://img.shields.io/youtube/channel/views/UCDh8zEDofOcrOMAOnSVL9Tg?label=Youtube&style=social)](https://www.youtube.com/@geminikims) | MCP:8Un92O | [![CI](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml) | MCP:8Un92O | [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-green.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | This is not the best structure. This is a good basic structure to use early in the project when productivity is important. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | Remember, as your software grows, your structure must grow too. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | # **Modules** | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## Core | MCP:8Un92O | Each submodule of this module is responsible for one domain service. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | This must make the modular structure grow with the growth of the service. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ### core:core-api | MCP:8Un92O | It is the only executable module in the project. It is structured to have domains to maximize initial development productivity. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | It is also responsible for providing APIs and setting up frameworks for services. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ### core:core-enum | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | This module contains enums that are used by `core-api` and must be delivered to external modules. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O |
| MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## Clients | MCP:8Un92O | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with external systems. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ### clients:clients-example | MCP:8Un92O | This module shows an example of HTTP communication with `Spring-Cloud-Open-Feign`. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O |
| MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## Storage | MCP:8Un92O | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with the various storages. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ### storage:db-core | MCP:8Un92O | This module shows an example of connecting to `MySql` using `Spring-Data-JPA`. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O |
| MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## Support | MCP:8Un92O | Submodules of this module are responsible for additional support. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ### support:logging | MCP:8Un92O | This module supports logging of service and has a dependency added for distributed tracing support. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | It also includes dependencies to support `Sentry`. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ### support:monitoring | MCP:8Un92O | This module supports monitoring of services. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O |
| MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## Tests | MCP:8Un92O | Submodules of this module are responsible for the convenience of writing test codes. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ### tests:api-docs | MCP:8Un92O | This module is for writing spring-rest-docs conveniently. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O |
| MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | # Dependency Management | MCP:8Un92O | All dependency versioning is done through `gradle.properties` file. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | If you want to add a new dependency, put the version in `gradle.properties` and load it in `build.gradle`. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O |
| MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | # Runtime Profiles | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## local | MCP:8Un92O | This profile aims to configure an environment that can be developed even if the network is disconnected. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## local-dev | MCP:8Un92O | This profile aims configurations that allow me to connect to the DEV environment from my local machine. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## dev | MCP:8Un92O | This profile exists for deploying Development environments. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## staging | MCP:8Un92O | This profile exists for deploying Staging environments. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## live | MCP:8Un92O | This profile exists for deploying Live environments. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O |
| MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | # Test Tasks & Tags | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## test | MCP:8Un92O | This is a collection of test-tasks that we want to run on `CI`. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | If you want to change the settings, modify the `build.gradle` file. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## unitTest | MCP:8Un92O | This is a group of tests that typically have no dependencies, are fast to run, and test a single feature. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## contextTest | MCP:8Un92O | This is a task that runs with SpringContext and has integration tests. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## restDocsTest | MCP:8Un92O | This is a task to create asciidoc based on spring-rest-docs. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## developTest | MCP:8Un92O | This is a task of tests that should not be run in `CI`. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | This is a good tag to use if you're not good at writing tests. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O |
| MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | # Recommended Preferences | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## Git Hook | MCP:8Un92O | This setting makes run `lint` on every commit. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ``` | MCP:8Un92O | $ git config core.hookspath .githooks | MCP:8Un92O | ``` | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ## IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:8Un92O | This setting makes it easier to run the `test code` out of the box. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | ``` | MCP:8Un92O | // Gradle Build and run with IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:8Un92O | Build, Execution, Deployment > Build Tools > Gradle > Run tests using > IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:8Un92O | ``` | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | If you want to apply lint settings to the format of IDEA, please refer to the guide below. | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | [Spring Java Format IntelliJ IDEA](https://github.com/spring-io/spring-javaformat#intellij-idea) | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | --- | MCP:8Un92O | | MCP:8Un92O | # Supported By | MCP:8Un92O |
| MCP:8Un92O | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: ae2fee1a95d4ef3b1bae148cbdbc4f0d9726b694)

| MCP:8Un92O | #!/bin/bash
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | GIT_DIR=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
| MCP:8Un92O | $GIT_DIR/gradlew checkFormat

| MCP:8Un92O | spring.application.name: core-api
| MCP:8Un92O | spring.profiles.active: local
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | spring:
| MCP:8Un92O |   config:
| MCP:8Un92O |     import:
| MCP:8Un92O |       - monitoring.yml
| MCP:8Un92O |       - logging.yml
| MCP:8Un92O |       - db-core.yml
| MCP:8Un92O |       - client-example.yml
| MCP:8Un92O |   web.resources.add-mappings: false
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | server:
| MCP:8Un92O |   tomcat:
| MCP:8Un92O |     max-connections: 20000
| MCP:8Un92O |     threads:
| MCP:8Un92O |       max: 600
| MCP:8Un92O |       min-spare: 100
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | ---
| MCP:8Un92O | spring.config.activate.on-profile: local
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | ---
| MCP:8Un92O | spring.config.activate.on-profile: local-dev
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | ---
| MCP:8Un92O | spring.config.activate.on-profile: dev
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | ---
| MCP:8Un92O | spring.config.activate.on-profile: staging
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | ---
| MCP:8Un92O | spring.config.activate.on-profile: live
| MCP:8Un92O |

| MCP:8Un92O | ### Application version ###
| MCP:8Un92O | applicationVersion=0.0.1-SNAPSHOT
| MCP:8Un92O | 
| MCP:8Un92O | ### Project configs ###
| MCP:8Un92O | projectGroup=io.dodn.springboot
| MCP:8Un92O | javaVersion=25

| MCP:8Un92O | asciidoctorConvertVersion=4.0.5
| MCP:8Un92O | springJavaFormatVersion=0.0.47
| MCP:8Un92O |

| MCP:8Un92O | springBootVersion=4.0.0

| MCP:8Un92O | springCloudDependenciesVersion=2025.1.0

data.content:
This is a **Spring Boot Java template** (`rpnr-bench/T03_R02`) that follows a multi-module Gradle structure. Below are the documented runtime profiles and the repository’s recommended developer setup for linting and testing locally.

---

## **Runtime Profiles**

The template defines five Spring profiles, each serving a distinct deployment or development purpose:

| Profile | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| **local** | Configured for offline development – works even without network connectivity.  
| **local-dev** | Allows connecting from your local machine to a shared **DEV** environment.  
| **dev** | Used for deploying the **Development** environment.  
| **staging** | Used for deploying the **Staging** environment.  
| **live** | Used for deploying the **Live** (production) environment.  

The default active profile is **local** (set in `core/core-api/src/main/resources/application.yml`).

---

## **Recommended Developer Setup for Linting & Testing Locally**

### **1. Linting (Code Formatting)**

- **Tool:** [Spring Java Format](https://github.com/spring-io/spring-javaformat) (version `0.0.47`, configured in `gradle.properties`).
- **Gradle command:** Run `./gradlew checkFormat` to validate formatting against the Spring Java Format rules.
- **Pre-commit hook:** The repository includes a Git pre-commit hook (`.githooks/pre-commit`) that automatically runs `./gradlew checkFormat` on every commit. To activate it, run:
  ```bash
  git config core.hookspath .githooks
  ```
- **IntelliJ IDEA integration:** Optionally, you can import the Spring Java Format settings into IntelliJ IDEA for real-time formatting. Follow the guide linked in the README: [Spring Java Format IntelliJ IDEA](https://github.com/spring-io/spring-javaformat#intellij-idea).

### **2. Running Tests Locally**

The template uses JUnit 5 with tag-based test segregation:

| Gradle Task | What it runs |
|-------------|--------------|
| `./gradlew test` | Runs all tests **except** those tagged `develop` and `restdocs`.  
| `./gradlew unitTest` | Runs fast, dependency-free unit tests (excludes `develop`, `context`, `restdocs` tags).  
| `./gradlew contextTest` | Runs integration tests that require a Spring context (`context` tag).  
| `./gradlew restDocsTest` | Generates AsciiDoc API documentation via Spring REST Docs (`restdocs` tag).  
| `./gradlew developTest` | Runs experimental/development-only tests (`develop` tag) – intended for local use, not CI.  

**IntelliJ IDEA recommendation:**  
Set *Build, Execution, Deployment → Build Tools → Gradle → Run tests using* to **IntelliJ IDEA**. This ensures test code runs seamlessly within the IDE.

### **3. Additional Notes**

- **Dependency management:** All versions are centralized in `gradle.properties`.
- **Lint configuration:** Applied globally via `lint.gradle`, which applies the `io.spring.javaformat` plugin to all subprojects.
- **CI badge:** The README includes a CI badge (GitHub Actions) that runs the `test` task (excluding `develop` and `restdocs` tags).

---

**In summary:** For local development, activate the Git hook for automatic linting, configure IntelliJ IDEA to use its own test runner, and use the appropriate Gradle task (`test`, `unitTest`, `contextTest`, etc.) based on what you want to validate.
